statement: When I'm not shipping code or running browser experiments, I'm usually behind a chessboard, on a long walk, or reading something I shouldn't be reading at 2 AM.

pursuits.title: Chess
pursuits.body: Tournament-style classical and rapid. Currently working through endgame studies — Dvoretsky's manual is on my desk most evenings.

pursuits.title: Competitive Programming
pursuits.body: 600+ problems on Codeforces & LeetCode. Same muscles I exercise when debugging a flaky Playwright test at 11 PM.

pursuits.title: Mechanical Keyboards
pursuits.body: Quiet linears and home-row mods. Typing comfortably for 10 hours a day is a real ergonomic concern, not a hobby.

pursuits.title: Writing & Technical Notes
pursuits.body: Building a habit of long-form writing — partly to think more clearly, partly as PhD-application practice.

pursuits.title: Tea & Slow Mornings
pursuits.body: Strong tea, no rush, paper notebook. The best research ideas have all shown up before 9 AM.

pursuits.title: Walks & Photography
pursuits.body: Old Dhaka, Ramna Park, anywhere with texture. Camera optional but encouraged.
